name: Fruit Leather
recipeIngredient: Ripe fruit of your choice (such as apricots, apples, grapes, berries, pineapple, oranges, pear, peaches, plums, tropical fruits, etc.)
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the fruit by removing stones, pits, seeds, and peel if desired.
2. Cut the fruit into chunks and place it in a blender to puree.
3. Line a cookie sheet or drying tray with Saran wrap or waxed paper. Make sure the pan has edges.
4. Pour the puree about 1/4-inch deep onto the lined sheet, distributing it evenly by tilting the pan. Do not use a spatula or knife.
5. Set the oven to its lowest setting (140°F).
6. Place the pan in the oven and crack open the door 2 to 6 inches. Allow the fruit leather to dry for 4 to 6 hours, until it is barely sticky to the touch and can easily peel off the Saran wrap.
7. After loosening the edge of the Saran wrap and peeling back an inch, roll the Saran wrap and fruit leather together into one piece (or cut into individual pieces).
8. Store the dried fruit leather at room temperature for up to 30 days, in the refrigerator for several months, or in the freezer for years.
